title: ProcScript for Managing Components
text: ProcScript for Managing Components Component-based development enables you to create multi-tier applications. There are various ProcScript instructions and functions for handling component-based processing. ProcScript instructions can: Create new instances of components Delete instances of components Identify which instances of a component are attached to the current component instance Identify which instance of a component activated the current component instance Return the component type of any given component instance Send synchronous and asynchronous messages to an instance of a component Pass parameters to an instance of a component Activate operations in other components Services, reports, and server pages can exist and be activated anywhere in the deployment infrastructure. Forms must be processed on the system where they are displayed, Table 1. ProcScript for Component-Based Development ProcScript command Description newinstance Deletes an instance of a component. activate Starts an operation on a specified component. deleteinstance Deletes an instance of a component. $componentname Return the name of the component from which the specified instance was created. $componenttype Return the type of the specified component instance. $instancechildren Return a list of instances attached to an instance. $instancedb Return an indication whether data in the current instance has been retrieved from a database. $instancedbmod Return the modification status of database fields in the current component instance. $instancehandle Return the handle of the requested instance. $instancemod Return the modification status of data in the current component instance. $instancename Return the name of the current component instance. $instancechildren Return a list of instances attached to an instance. $instanceparent Return the name of the parent instance of the requested instance. Related Links Component Memory Management At runtime, component instances require memory to handle the component definition and component data. This memory is not necessarily freed when the component instance is deleted. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
